title: Sri Rama Raksha Stotram
about: Composed by the sage Budhakaushika, this stotram calls on Rama's names for protection. Its kavacha (armor) section names a different epithet of Rama for each part of the body. Daily recitation is believed to dispel fear and bring peace of mind.

entries.meaning: The central name of the whole stotram; protects the entire body in the kavacha
Rama's wife, Janaki
Born in the Raghu dynasty; protects the head in the kavacha
Son of Dasharatha
Sita's husband; protects the hands in the kavacha
The monkey king, Rama's ally, named in the 'Jayatyatibalo Ramo' verse
Dasharatha's fourth son, Rama's brother
The sage who composed this stotram; named as its rishi
Son of the wind god, devoted to Rama; named the 'kilaka' (key) of the stotram
Dasharatha's son, Rama's brother, son of Sumitra
Daughter of King Janaka, Sita
The serene one, named in the meditation verse
Son of Kausalya; protects the eyes in the kavacha
Beloved of Janaki (Sita)
Joy of the Raghu dynasty
Elder brother of Bharata
Formidable in battle
Son of Dasharatha; protects the forehead in the kavacha
Honored by Bharata; protects the throat in the kavacha
Best of the Raghu dynasty; protects the thighs in the kavacha
Builder of the bridge across the ocean; protects the knees in the kavacha
Protector of the sacrifice; protects the nose in the kavacha
A treasure of learning; protects the tongue in the kavacha
Bearer of divine weapons; protects the shoulders in the kavacha
Lord of Sugriva; protects the waist in the kavacha
The greatly strong one - Lakshmana's epithet in the 'Jayatyatibalo Ramo' verse
Lord of the Kosala kingdom (Rama)
Ravana's brother, the Lanka king who took refuge with Rama
The name the stotram itself gives its armor - a cage of thunderbolts
The meter in which this stotram is composed
The auspicious, gentle Rama
A number-name meaning Raghava's story spans a hundred crore verses
Dear to sage Vishvamitra; protects the ears in the kavacha
Affectionate toward Lakshmana, son of Sumitra; protects the face in the kavacha
Breaker of Shiva's bow; protects the arms in the kavacha
Conqueror of Parashurama; protects the heart in the kavacha
Destroyer of the demon Khara; protects the midsection in the kavacha
Refuge of Jambavan; protects the navel in the kavacha
Lord of Hanuman; protects the knee joints in the kavacha
Ender of the ten-headed Ravana; protects the calves in the kavacha
Granter of kingship to Vibhishana; protects the feet in the kavacha
The sacrifice equal in merit to reciting this stotram, per the phala shruti
